document.title: Rietveld analysis of structure transformation between perovskite-type and B-type rare earth structures

figure.caption: Fig. 6 Fig.6XRD patterns of LaLnO3 prepared by firing at 1400℃.

analysis_paragraphs.text: 10 20 30 40 50 60 70 80 2θ(deg.) Intensity (a.u.) 112 200 020 111 110 132 204 220 311 224 313 221 212 121 225 332 116 1200℃-10 h 1400℃-10 h 202 111 -402 003 -112 -313 -511 600 313 -801 -425 -207 404 711 421 023 -206 -910 -1003 Rwp = 10.5% S = 1.73 Orthorho mbic Monoclinic B-type ra re S.G.：Pnma 96.4 3.6 S.G.：C 2/m In the LaLnO3 systems, when Ln = Er (0.890Å ) or Yb (0868Å ) which has the smaller the ionic radius than that of Y 3+ , the sample showed the orthorhombic perovskite-type structure regardless 1400℃ or 1600℃ firing, while when Ln = Dy, (0.912Å ) or Ho (0.901Å ) which has larger the ionic radius than that of Y 3+ , it showed the monoclinic B-type rare earth structure regardless the firing temperatures, as shown in Fig. 6 and 7 . These facts suggest that the crystal structure of LaLnO3 system strongly dependents on the ionic size of Ln 3+ .

Intensity (a.u.) 112 200 020 111 110 132 204 220 311 224 313 221 212 121 225 332 116 1200℃-10 h 1400℃-10 h 202 111 -402 003 -112 -313 -511 600 313 -801 -425 -207 404 711 421 023 -206 -910 -1003 Rwp = 10.5% S = 1.73 Orthorho mbic Monoclinic B-type ra re S.G.：Pnma 96.4 3.6 S.G.：C 2/m In the LaLnO3 systems, when Ln = Er (0.890Å ) or Yb (0868Å ) which has the smaller the ionic radius than that of Y 3+ , the sample showed the orthorhombic perovskite-type structure regardless 1400℃ or 1600℃ firing, while when Ln = Dy, (0.912Å ) or Ho (0.901Å ) which has larger the ionic radius than that of Y 3+ , it showed the monoclinic B-type rare earth structure regardless the firing temperatures, as shown in Fig. 6 and 7 .

combined_analysis_text:
10 20 30 40 50 60 70 80 2θ(deg.) Intensity (a.u.) 112 200 020 111 110 132 204 220 311 224 313 221 212 121 225 332 116 1200℃-10 h 1400℃-10 h 202 111 -402 003 -112 -313 -511 600 313 -801 -425 -207 404 711 421 023 -206 -910 -1003 Rwp = 10.5% S = 1.73 Orthorho mbic Monoclinic B-type ra re S.G.：Pnma 96.4 3.6 S.G.：C 2/m In the LaLnO3 systems, when Ln = Er (0.890Å ) or Yb (0868Å ) which has the smaller the ionic radius than that of Y 3+ , the sample showed the orthorhombic perovskite-type structure regardless 1400℃ or 1600℃ firing, while when Ln = Dy, (0.912Å ) or Ho (0.901Å ) which has larger the ionic radius than that of Y 3+ , it showed the monoclinic B-type rare earth structure regardless the firing temperatures, as shown in Fig. 6 and 7 .

Furthermore, LaErxHo1-xO3 system was also produced same as average ionic radius of (Ln, Ln') in LaHoxYb1-xO3 system, and LaErxHo1-xO3 system was performed Rietveld analysis. As a result, when average ionic radius of (Ln, Ln') which has smaller than rav = 0.8977Å , the sample showed the orthorhombic perovskite-type structure, while when average ionic radius of (Ln, Ln') which has larger than rav = 0.8994 Å , it showed the monoclinic B-type rare earth structure. Therefore, in the La(Ln, Ln')O3 systems, controlling for the average ionic radius of (Ln, Ln'), we could produce the orthorhombic perovskite-type or monoclinic B-type rare earth structures.

document.title: Rietveld analysis of structure transformation between perovskite-type and B-type rare earth structures

figure.caption: Fig. 8 Fig.8XRD patterns of LaHoxYb1-xO3 firing 1400℃ and annealed at 1300℃.

analysis_paragraphs.text: In the system LaHoxYb1-xO3 (0.90≦ x≦ 0.95), XRD patterns showed mixed phases of the monoclinic B-type rare earth and orthorhombic perovskite-type structures when the samples were sintered at 1400 ℃ for 10 h. Therefore, the samples were annealed at 1300℃ for 10 h. The samples with x = 0.90 (rav.=0.8977Å ) showed the orthorhombic perovskite-type structure, and then changed to the mixed phases of the orthorhombic perovskite-type and monoclinic B-type rare earth structures in the composition range, 0.90<x≦ 0.95, as shown in Fig. 8 . Fig. 9 shows XRD patterns of LaHoxYb1-xO3 system prepared by firing at 1600 ℃ for 20 h. The samples with x = 0.90 (rav.=0.8977 Å ) showed the orthorhombic perovskite-type structure by firing at 1600℃ for 20 h, and then changed to the mixed phases of the monoclinic B-type rare earth and orthorhombic perovskite-type structures in the composition range, 0.90<x<0.95, and when x = 0.95 (rav.=0.8994 Å ), the sample showed the monoclinic B-type rare earth structure.
analysis_paragraphs.relevant_text: The samples with x = 0.90 (rav.=0.8977Å ) showed the orthorhombic perovskite-type structure, and then changed to the mixed phases of the orthorhombic perovskite-type and monoclinic B-type rare earth structures in the composition range, 0.90<x≦ 0.95, as shown in Fig. 8 .

combined_analysis_text:
The samples with x = 0.90 (rav.=0.8977Å ) showed the orthorhombic perovskite-type structure, and then changed to the mixed phases of the orthorhombic perovskite-type and monoclinic B-type rare earth structures in the composition range, 0.90<x≦ 0.95, as shown in Fig. 8 .

Furthermore, LaErxHo1-xO3 system was also produced same as average ionic radius of (Ln, Ln') in LaHoxYb1-xO3 system, and LaErxHo1-xO3 system was performed Rietveld analysis. As a result, when average ionic radius of (Ln, Ln') which has smaller than rav = 0.8977Å , the sample showed the orthorhombic perovskite-type structure, while when average ionic radius of (Ln, Ln') which has larger than rav = 0.8994 Å , it showed the monoclinic B-type rare earth structure. Therefore, in the La(Ln, Ln')O3 systems, controlling for the average ionic radius of (Ln, Ln'), we could produce the orthorhombic perovskite-type or monoclinic B-type rare earth structures.

document.title: Rietveld analysis of structure transformation between perovskite-type and B-type rare earth structures

figure.caption: Fig. 9 Fig.9XRD patterns of LaHoxYb1-xO3 prepared by firing at 1600℃.

analysis_paragraphs.text: In the system LaHoxYb1-xO3 (0.90≦ x≦ 0.95), XRD patterns showed mixed phases of the monoclinic B-type rare earth and orthorhombic perovskite-type structures when the samples were sintered at 1400 ℃ for 10 h. Therefore, the samples were annealed at 1300℃ for 10 h. The samples with x = 0.90 (rav.=0.8977Å ) showed the orthorhombic perovskite-type structure, and then changed to the mixed phases of the orthorhombic perovskite-type and monoclinic B-type rare earth structures in the composition range, 0.90<x≦ 0.95, as shown in Fig. 8 . Fig. 9 shows XRD patterns of LaHoxYb1-xO3 system prepared by firing at 1600 ℃ for 20 h. The samples with x = 0.90 (rav.=0.8977 Å ) showed the orthorhombic perovskite-type structure by firing at 1600℃ for 20 h, and then changed to the mixed phases of the monoclinic B-type rare earth and orthorhombic perovskite-type structures in the composition range, 0.90<x<0.95, and when x = 0.95 (rav.=0.8994 Å ), the sample showed the monoclinic B-type rare earth structure.
analysis_paragraphs.relevant_text: Fig. 9 shows XRD patterns of LaHoxYb1-xO3 system prepared by firing at 1600 ℃ for 20 h.

combined_analysis_text:
Fig. 9 shows XRD patterns of LaHoxYb1-xO3 system prepared by firing at 1600 ℃ for 20 h.

Furthermore, LaErxHo1-xO3 system was also produced same as average ionic radius of (Ln, Ln') in LaHoxYb1-xO3 system, and LaErxHo1-xO3 system was performed Rietveld analysis. As a result, when average ionic radius of (Ln, Ln') which has smaller than rav = 0.8977Å , the sample showed the orthorhombic perovskite-type structure, while when average ionic radius of (Ln, Ln') which has larger than rav = 0.8994 Å , it showed the monoclinic B-type rare earth structure. Therefore, in the La(Ln, Ln')O3 systems, controlling for the average ionic radius of (Ln, Ln'), we could produce the orthorhombic perovskite-type or monoclinic B-type rare earth structures.

With increasing average ionic radius of B site, phase existence ratio was obtained linearly decreasing trend for orthorhombic perovskite-type structure, and it was obtained linearly increasing trend for monoclinic B-type rare earth structure.
